revision.text: #redirect[[en:Nashitomi]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 梨迹臣命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]]、上古 | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 梨富命、梨津臣命、那志登美 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = | 氏族 = [[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[伊賀津臣命]]、母：豊幡媛命 | 兄弟 = 臣知人命、伊世理命、奈是理比売 | 妻 = 冨炊屋媛命、玉岐姫命 | 子 = 建稲穂命、建御世狭名命、兄勝命、弟勝命、[[神聞勝命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''梨迹臣命'''（なしとみのみこと、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]で'''[[中臣氏|中臣連]]'''の祖。'''梨富命'''、'''那志登美'''<ref name="fudoki">秋元吉郎『日本古典文学体系新装版 風土記』[[岩波書店]]、1993年、457、458頁。</ref>、'''梨津臣命'''（なしつおみのみこと）とも表記する。
== 概要 == 『[[記紀]]』に登場せず特に事績も知られないが、『[[近江国風土記]]』に掲載された[[余呉湖]]の[[羽衣伝説]]おいて、[[近江国]][[伊香郡]]与胡郷の人である[[伊賀津臣命|伊香刀美]]が、天女との間に生んだ二男二女の次男として登場している<ref name="fudoki"/>。
近江国[[式内社]]の乃彌神社の祭神とされ、合祀した[[乎彌神社]]において兄弟の臣知人命と共に祀られている。
== 系譜 == 父は[[伊賀津臣命]]、母は彦羽冨命の娘である豊幡媛命で、[[額田部氏|額田部連]]の祖・坂戸毘古命の娘である冨炊屋媛命を娶って、飛鳥直の祖・建稲穂命、伊予部連の祖・建御世狭名命、武蔵・伊豆[[占部]]、[[卜部氏|卜部朝臣]]の祖・兄勝命、殖栗連、常陸占部の祖・弟勝命、[[中臣氏|中臣連]]等の祖・[[神聞勝命]]の五男子を生んだとする系図がある<ref>[[中田憲信]]「赤堀」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「青木」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>[[鈴木真年]]「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref>。ただし、兄勝命・弟勝命の兄弟は玉祖連の祖・久志伊麻命の娘である玉岐姫命との子であるとする系図もある<ref>中田憲信「兼松」『諸系譜』第二十四冊。</ref>。
母・豊幡媛命の父である彦羽冨命の出自については[[三上氏]]族の川枯彦命と同人か近親と見られるが、梨迹臣命の子として建稲穂命の名が見えることは、伝承の舞台に白鳥が稲の穀霊神として飛来し、肥沃な土地であるとする見方や、三上氏など[[天孫族]]が鳥[[トーテミズム]]や天降り伝承を持つことに通じるとする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「五 崇神前代の初期分岐」『古代氏族の研究⑤ 中臣氏 卜占を担った古代占部の後裔』青垣出版、2014年、95頁。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[卜部]] *[[占部氏]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{デフォルトソート:かみききかつのみこと}} [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:近江国の人物]] [[Category:中臣氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

母・豊幡媛命の父である彦羽冨命の出自については[[三上氏]]族の川枯彦命と同人か近親と見られるが、梨迹臣命の子として建稲穂命の名が見えることは、伝承の舞台に白鳥が稲の穀霊神として飛来し、肥沃な土地であるとする見方や、三上氏など[[天孫族]]が鳥[[トーテミズム]]や天降り伝承を持つことに通じるとする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「五 崇神前代の初期分岐」『古代氏族の研究⑤ 中臣氏 卜占を担った古代占部の後裔』青垣出版、2014年、95頁。</ref>。

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 梨迹臣命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]]、上古 | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 梨富命、梨津臣命、那志登美 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = | 氏族 = [[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[伊賀津臣命]]、母：豊幡媛命 | 兄弟 = 臣知人命、伊世理命、奈是理比売 | 妻 = 冨炊屋媛命、玉岐姫命 | 子 = 建稲穂命、建御世狭名命、兄勝命、弟勝命、[[神聞勝命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''梨迹臣命'''（なしとみのみこと、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]で'''[[中臣氏|中臣連]]'''の祖。'''梨富命'''、'''那志登美'''<ref name="fudoki">秋元吉郎『日本古典文学体系新装版 風土記』[[岩波書店]]、1993年、457、458頁。</ref>、'''梨津臣命'''（なしつおみのみこと）とも表記する。
== 概要 == 『[[記紀]]』に登場せず特に事績も知られないが、『[[近江国風土記]]』に掲載された[[余呉湖]]の[[羽衣伝説]]おいて、[[近江国]][[伊香郡]]与胡郷の人である[[伊賀津臣命|伊香刀美]]が、天女との間に生んだ二男二女の次男として登場している<ref name="fudoki"/>。
近江国[[式内社]]の乃彌神社の祭神とされ、合祀した[[乎彌神社]]において兄弟の臣知人命と共に祀られている。その他に意富布良神社にも配祀されている。
== 系譜 == 父は[[伊賀津臣命]]である。
飛鳥直の祖・建稲穂命、伊予部連の祖・建御世狭名命、武蔵・伊豆[[占部]]、[[卜部氏|卜部朝臣]]の祖・兄勝命、殖栗連、常陸占部の祖・弟勝命、[[中臣氏|中臣連]]等の祖・[[神聞勝命]]の五男子を生んだとする系図がある<ref>[[中田憲信]]「赤堀」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「青木」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>[[鈴木真年]]「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref>。ただし、兄勝命・弟勝命の兄弟は玉祖連の祖・久志伊麻命の娘である玉岐姫命との子であるとする系図もある<ref>中田憲信「兼松」『諸系譜』第二十四冊。</ref>。これらの系図は近世のものであり、信憑性は低く、学界では認められていない。
=== 宝賀寿男の異説 === 母とされる豊幡媛命の父である彦羽冨命の出自については[[三上氏]]族の川枯彦命と同人か近親と見られるが、梨迹臣命の子として建稲穂命の名が見えることは、伝承の舞台に白鳥が稲の穀霊神として飛来し、肥沃な土地であるとする見方や、三上氏など[[天孫族]]が鳥[[トーテミズム]]や天降り伝承を持つことに通じるとする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「五 崇神前代の初期分岐」『古代氏族の研究⑤ 中臣氏 卜占を担った古代占部の後裔』青垣出版、2014年、95頁。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[卜部]] *[[占部氏]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{デフォルトソート:なしとみのみこと}} [[Category:中臣氏]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:近江国の人物]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 梨迹臣命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]]、上古 | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 梨富命、梨津臣命、那志登美 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = | 氏族 = [[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[伊賀津臣命]] | 兄弟 = 意美志留、'''那志登美'''、伊是理比咩、奈是理比売 | 妻 = 冨炊屋媛命 | 子 =[[神聞勝命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''梨迹臣命'''（なしとみのみこと、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]で'''[[中臣氏|中臣連]]'''の祖。'''梨富命'''、'''那志登美'''<ref name="fudoki">秋元吉郎『日本古典文学体系新装版 風土記』[[岩波書店]]、1993年、457、458頁。</ref>、'''梨津臣命'''（なしつおみのみこと）とも表記する。
== 概要 == 『[[記紀]]』に登場せず特に事績も知られないが、『[[近江国風土記]]』に掲載された[[余呉湖]]の[[羽衣伝説]]おいて、[[近江国]][[伊香郡]]与胡郷の人である[[伊賀津臣命|伊香刀美]]が、天女との間に生んだ二男二女の次男として登場している<ref name="fudoki"/>。
近江国[[式内社]]の乃彌神社の祭神とされ、合祀した[[乎彌神社]]において兄弟の臣知人命と共に祀られている。その他に意富布良神社にも配祀されている。
== 系譜 == 父は[[伊賀津臣命]]である。子孫は[[中臣氏]]、[[大中臣氏]]、[[藤原氏]]などになった。
また、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によれば、[[河内国]]川跨連の祖であるという。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[卜部]] *[[占部氏]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{デフォルトソート:なしとみのみこと}} [[Category:中臣氏]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:近江国の人物]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Nashitomi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]], ancient Japan | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, Nashitomi | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命}} | siblings = Omishiru, Nashitomi, {{ill|Iserihime|ja|伊是理比咩}}, Nasirihime | spouse = {{ill|Tomiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | issue = {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命}} }}
'''Nashitomi no Mikoto''' (梨迹臣命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese noble and ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}. He is also known by the names Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, and Nashitomi.
== Overview == Although Nashitomi no Mikoto does not appear in the *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}* or *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}*, he is mentioned in the *{{ill|Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|近江国風土記}}*. According to the text, he was the second son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命}}, who had two sons and two daughters with a celestial maiden in the {{ill|Hagoromo|ja|羽衣伝説}} associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}, Ikagō District.<ref name="fudoki">Yoshirō Akimoto, *Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei Shinsōban Fudoki* (Iwanami Shoten, 1993), pp. 457-458.</ref>
Nashitomi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at the *{{ill|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|lt=Noimi Shrine|ja|乃彌神社}}*, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in Ōmi Province, where he is worshipped alongside his brother Omishiru no Mikoto at the *{{ill|lt=Omi Shrine|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja|乎彌神社}}*, which incorporated Noimi Shrine. Additionally, he is also enshrined at *{{ill|Ōfura Shrine|ja|意富布良神社}}* as a subordinate deity.
== Genealogy == Nashitomi no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命}} and father of {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命}}. His descendants include prominent clans such as the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏}}, and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}}.
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}* identifies him as the ancestor of the Kawamatauji no Muraji of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Radical 25|Radical 25]] * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|clan Urabe}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

According to the text, he was the second son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=draft}}, who had two sons and two daughters with a celestial maiden in the {{ill|Hagoromo|ja|羽衣伝説|12=draft}} associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|12=draft}}, Ikagō District.<ref name="fudoki">Yoshirō Akimoto, *Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei Shinsōban Fudoki* (Iwanami Shoten, 1993), pp. 457-458.</ref>
Nashitomi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at the *{{ill|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|lt=Noimi Shrine|ja|乃彌神社|12=draft}}*, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} in Ōmi Province, where he is worshipped alongside his brother Omishiru no Mikoto at the *{{ill|lt=Omi Shrine|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja|乎彌神社|12=draft}}*, which incorporated Noimi Shrine. Additionally, he is also enshrined at *{{ill|Ōfura Shrine|ja|意富布良神社|12=draft}}* as a subordinate deity.
== Genealogy == Nashitomi no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|12=draft}}. His descendants include prominent clans such as the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|12=draft}}.
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}* identifies him as the ancestor of the Kawamatauji no Muraji of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Radical 25|Radical 25]] * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|clan Urabe}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}}

12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:梨迹臣命 up to rev 88541870
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 梨迹臣命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]]、上古 | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 梨富命、梨津臣命、那志登美 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = | 氏族 = [[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[伊賀津臣命]] | 兄弟 = 意美志留、'''那志登美'''、伊是理比咩、奈是理比売 | 妻 = 冨炊屋媛命 | 子 =[[神聞勝命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''梨迹臣命'''（なしとみのみこと、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]で'''[[中臣氏|中臣連]]'''の祖。'''梨富命'''、'''那志登美'''<ref name="fudoki">秋元吉郎『日本古典文学体系新装版 風土記』[[岩波書店]]、1993年、457、458頁。</ref>、'''梨津臣命'''（なしつおみのみこと）とも表記する。
== 概要 == 『[[記紀]]』に登場せず特に事績も知られないが、『[[近江国風土記]]』に掲載された[[余呉湖]]の[[羽衣伝説]]おいて、[[近江国]][[伊香郡]]与胡郷の人である[[伊賀津臣命|伊香刀美]]が、天女との間に生んだ二男二女の次男として登場している<ref name="fudoki"/>。
近江国[[式内社]]の乃彌神社の祭神とされ、合祀した[[乎彌神社]]において兄弟の臣知人命と共に祀られている。その他に意富布良神社にも配祀されている。
== 系譜 == 父は[[伊賀津臣命]]である。子孫は[[中臣氏]]、[[大中臣氏]]、[[藤原氏]]などになった。
また、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によれば、[[河内国]]川跨連の祖であるという。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[卜部]] *[[占部氏]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{デフォルトソート:なしとみのみこと}} [[Category:中臣氏]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:近江国の人物]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梨迹臣命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Kamikikikatsu|qid=Q106106971}}|father={{ill|Ikatsuomikoto|qid=Q109599098}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q108616765]] Wikidata name: Nashitomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Nashitomi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]], ancient Japan | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, Nashitomi | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} | siblings = Omishiru, Nashitomi, {{ill|Iserihime|ja|伊是理比咩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iserihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nasirihime | spouse = {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579611}} | issue = {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}} }}
'''Nashitomi no Mikoto''' (梨迹臣命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese noble and ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. He is also known by the names Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, and Nashitomi.
== Overview == Although Nashitomi no Mikoto does not appear in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]* or *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, he is mentioned in the *{{ill|Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|近江国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}*. According to the text, he was the second son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}}, who had two sons and two daughters with a celestial maiden in the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|qq=|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]], Ikagō District.<ref name="fudoki">Yoshirō Akimoto, *Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei Shinsōban Fudoki* (Iwanami Shoten, 1993), pp. 457-458.</ref>
Nashitomi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at the *{{ill|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|lt=Noimi Shrine|ja|乃彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Shrine (Yogo)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乎彌神社}}*, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Ōmi Province, where he is worshipped alongside his brother Omishiru no Mikoto at the *{{ill|lt=Omi Shrine|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja|乎彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lt=Omi Shrine|qq=}}*, which incorporated Noimi Shrine. Additionally, he is also enshrined at *{{ill|Ōfura Shrine|ja|意富布良神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōfura Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* as a subordinate deity.
== Genealogy == Nashitomi no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}}. His descendants include prominent clans such as the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]], {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}, and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]].
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* identifies him as the ancestor of the Kawamatauji no Muraji of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Radical 25|Radical 25]] * {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|clan Urabe|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梨迹臣命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Kamikikikatsu|WD=Q106106971}}|father={{ill|Ikatsuomikoto|WD=Q109599098}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q108616765]] Wikidata name: Nashitomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Nashitomi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]], ancient Japan | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, Nashitomi | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} | siblings = Omishiru, Nashitomi, {{ill|Iserihime|ja|伊是理比咩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iserihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nasirihime | spouse = {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579611}} | issue = {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}} }}
'''Nashitomi no Mikoto''' (梨迹臣命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese noble and ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. He is also known by the names Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, and Nashitomi.
== Overview == Although Nashitomi no Mikoto does not appear in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]* or *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, he is mentioned in the *{{ill|Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|近江国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}*. According to the text, he was the second son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}}, who had two sons and two daughters with a celestial maiden in the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|qq=|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]], Ikagō District.<ref name="fudoki">Yoshirō Akimoto, *Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei Shinsōban Fudoki* (Iwanami Shoten, 1993), pp. 457-458.</ref>
Nashitomi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at the *{{ill|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|lt=Noimi Shrine|ja|乃彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Shrine (Yogo)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乎彌神社}}*, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Ōmi Province, where he is worshipped alongside his brother Omishiru no Mikoto at the *{{ill|lt=Omi Shrine|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja|乎彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lt=Omi Shrine|qq=}}*, which incorporated Noimi Shrine. Additionally, he is also enshrined at *{{ill|Ōfura Shrine|ja|意富布良神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōfura Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* as a subordinate deity.
== Genealogy == Nashitomi no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}}. His descendants include prominent clans such as the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]], {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}, and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]].
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* identifies him as the ancestor of the Kawamatauji no Muraji of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梨迹臣命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Kamikikikatsu|WD=Q106106971}}|father={{ill|Ikatsuomikoto|WD=Q109599098}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q108616765]] Wikidata name: Nashitomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Nashitomi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]], ancient Japan | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, Nashitomi | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} | siblings = Omishiru, Nashitomi, {{ill|Iserihime|ja|伊是理比咩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iserihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nasirihime | spouse = {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579611}} | issue = {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}} }}
'''Nashitomi no Mikoto''' (梨迹臣命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese noble and ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. He is also known by the names Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, and Nashitomi.
== Overview == Although Nashitomi no Mikoto does not appear in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]* or *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, he is mentioned in the *{{ill|Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|近江国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}*. According to the text, he was the second son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}}, who had two sons and two daughters with a celestial maiden in the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]], Ikagō District.<ref name="fudoki">Yoshirō Akimoto, *Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei Shinsōban Fudoki* (Iwanami Shoten, 1993), pp. 457-458.</ref>
Nashitomi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at the *{{ill|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|lt=Noimi Shrine|ja|乃彌神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乎彌神社}}*, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Ōmi Province, where he is worshipped alongside his brother Omishiru no Mikoto at the *{{ill|lt=Omi Shrine|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja|乎彌神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lt=Omi Shrine|qq=}}*, which incorporated Noimi Shrine. Additionally, he is also enshrined at *{{ill|Ōfura Shrine|ja|意富布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōfura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* as a subordinate deity.
== Genealogy == Nashitomi no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}}. His descendants include prominent clans such as the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]], {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}, and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]].
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* identifies him as the ancestor of the Kawamatauji no Muraji of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Radical 25|Radical 25]] * {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|clan Urabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}
{{translated page|ja|梨迹臣命|version=88541870|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梨迹臣命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Kamikikikatsu|WD=Q106106971}}|father={{ill|Ikatsuomikoto|WD=Q109599098}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q108616765]] Wikidata name: Nashitomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Nashitomi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]], ancient Japan | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, Nashitomi | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} | siblings = Omishiru, Nashitomi, {{ill|Iserihime|ja|伊是理比咩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iserihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nasirihime | spouse = {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579611}} | issue = {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}} }}
'''Nashitomi no Mikoto''' (梨迹臣命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese noble and ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. He is also known by the names Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, and Nashitomi.
== Overview == Although Nashitomi no Mikoto does not appear in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]* or *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, he is mentioned in the *{{ill|Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|近江国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}*. According to the text, he was the second son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}}, who had two sons and two daughters with a celestial maiden in the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]], Ikagō District.<ref name="fudoki">Yoshirō Akimoto, *Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei Shinsōban Fudoki* (Iwanami Shoten, 1993), pp. 457-458.</ref>
Nashitomi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at the *{{ill|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|lt=Noimi Shrine|ja|乃彌神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乎彌神社}}*, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Ōmi Province, where he is worshipped alongside his brother Omishiru no Mikoto at the *{{ill|lt=Omi Shrine|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja|乎彌神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lt=Omi Shrine|qq=}}*, which incorporated Noimi Shrine. Additionally, he is also enshrined at *{{ill|Ōfura Shrine|ja|意富布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōfura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* as a subordinate deity.
== Genealogy == Nashitomi no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}}. His descendants include prominent clans such as the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]], {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}, and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]].
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* identifies him as the ancestor of the Kawamatauji no Muraji of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Radical 25|Radical 25]] * {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|clan Urabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梨迹臣命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梨迹臣命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梨迹臣命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Kamikikikatsu|WD=Q106106971}}|father={{ill|Ikatsuomikoto|WD=Q109599098}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q108616765]] Wikidata name: Nashitomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Nashitomi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]], ancient Japan | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, Nashitomi | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} | siblings = Omishiru, Nashitomi, {{ill|Iserihime|ja|伊是理比咩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iserihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nasirihime | spouse = {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579611}} | issue = {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}} }}
'''Nashitomi no Mikoto''' (梨迹臣命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese noble and ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. He is also known by the names Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, and Nashitomi.
== Overview == Although Nashitomi no Mikoto does not appear in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]* or *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, he is mentioned in the *{{ill|Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|近江国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}*. According to the text, he was the second son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}}, who had two sons and two daughters with a celestial maiden in the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]], Ikagō District.<ref name="fudoki">Yoshirō Akimoto, *Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei Shinsōban Fudoki* (Iwanami Shoten, 1993), pp. 457-458.</ref>
Nashitomi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at the *{{ill|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|lt=Noimi Shrine|ja|乃彌神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乎彌神社}}*, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Ōmi Province, where he is worshipped alongside his brother Omishiru no Mikoto at the *{{ill|lt=Omi Shrine|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja|乎彌神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lt=Omi Shrine|qq=}}*, which incorporated Noimi Shrine. Additionally, he is also enshrined at *{{ill|Ōfura Shrine|ja|意富布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōfura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* as a subordinate deity.
== Genealogy == Nashitomi no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}}. His descendants include prominent clans such as the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]], {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}, and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]].
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* identifies him as the ancestor of the Kawamatauji no Muraji of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Radical 25|Radical 25]] * {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|clan Urabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梨迹臣命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梨迹臣命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梨迹臣命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Kamikikikatsu|WD=Q106106971}}|father={{ill|Ikatsuomikoto|WD=Q109599098}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q108616765]] Wikidata name: Nashitomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Nashitomi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]], ancient Japan | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, Nashitomi | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} | siblings = Omishiru, Nashitomi, {{ill|Iserihime|ja|伊是理比咩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iserihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nasirihime | spouse = {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579611}} | issue = {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}} }}
'''Nashitomi no Mikoto''' (梨迹臣命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese noble and ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. He is also known by the names Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, and Nashitomi.
== Overview == Although Nashitomi no Mikoto does not appear in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]* or *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, he is mentioned in the *{{ill|Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|近江国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}*. According to the text, he was the second son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}}, who had two sons and two daughters with a celestial maiden in the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]], Ikagō District.<ref name="fudoki">Yoshirō Akimoto, *Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei Shinsōban Fudoki* (Iwanami Shoten, 1993), pp. 457-458.</ref>
Nashitomi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at the *{{ill|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|lt=Noimi Shrine|ja|乃彌神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乎彌神社}}*, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Ōmi Province, where he is worshipped alongside his brother Omishiru no Mikoto at the *{{ill|lt=Omi Shrine|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja|乎彌神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lt=Omi Shrine|qq=}}*, which incorporated Noimi Shrine. Additionally, he is also enshrined at *{{ill|Ōfura Shrine|ja|意富布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōfura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* as a subordinate deity.
== Genealogy == Nashitomi no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}}. His descendants include prominent clans such as the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]], {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}, and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]].
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* identifies him as the ancestor of the Kawamatauji no Muraji of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Radical 25|Radical 25]] * {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|clan Urabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

== Genealogy == Nashitomi no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}}. His descendants include prominent clans such as the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}}, {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}}.
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}* identifies him as the ancestor of the Kawamatauji no Muraji of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梨迹臣命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梨迹臣命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梨迹臣命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Kamikikikatsu|WD=Q106106971}}|father={{ill|Ikatsuomikoto|WD=Q109599098}}}} {{Infobox noble | name = Nashitomi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]], ancient Japan | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, Nashitomi | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} | siblings = Omishiru, Nashitomi, {{ill|Iserihime|ja|伊是理比咩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iserihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nasirihime | spouse = {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579611}} | issue = {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}} }}
'''Nashitomi no Mikoto''' (梨迹臣命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese noble and ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}}. He is also known by the names Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, and Nashitomi.
== Overview == Although Nashitomi no Mikoto does not appear in the *{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}* or *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}*, he is mentioned in the *{{ill|Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|近江国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}*. According to the text, he was the second son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}}, who had two sons and two daughters with a celestial maiden in the {{ill|Swan maiden|en|Swan maiden|WD=Q1326754|lt=Hagoromo}} associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}}, Ikagō District.<ref name="fudoki">Yoshirō Akimoto, *Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei Shinsōban Fudoki* (Iwanami Shoten, 1993), pp. 457-458.</ref>
Nashitomi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at the *{{ill|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|lt=Noimi Shrine|ja|乃彌神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乎彌神社}}*, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Ōmi Province, where he is worshipped alongside his brother Omishiru no Mikoto at the *{{ill|lt=Omi Shrine|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja|乎彌神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lt=Omi Shrine|qq=}}*, which incorporated Noimi Shrine. Additionally, he is also enshrined at *{{ill|Ōfura Shrine|ja|意富布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōfura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* as a subordinate deity.
== Genealogy == Nashitomi no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}}. His descendants include prominent clans such as the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}}, {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}}.
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}* identifies him as the ancestor of the Kawamatauji no Muraji of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Radical 25|en|Radical 25|WD=Q94724|lt=Radical 25}} * {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|clan Urabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

'''Nashitomi no Mikoto''' (梨迹臣命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese noble and ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}}. He is also known by the names Nashifu no Mikoto, Nashitsu no Mikoto, and Nashitomi.
== Overview == Although Nashitomi no Mikoto does not appear in the *{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}* or *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}*, he is mentioned in the *{{ill|Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|近江国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}*. According to the text, he was the second son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}}, who had two sons and two daughters with a celestial maiden in the {{ill|Swan maiden|en|Swan maiden|WD=Q1326754|lt=Hagoromo|1=Swan maiden}} associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}, Ikagō District.<ref name="fudoki">Yoshirō Akimoto, *Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei Shinsōban Fudoki* (Iwanami Shoten, 1993), pp. 457-458.</ref>
Nashitomi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at the *{{ill|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|lt=Noimi Shrine|ja|乃彌神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乎彌神社}}*, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Ōmi Province, where he is worshipped alongside his brother Omishiru no Mikoto at the *{{ill|lt=Omi Shrine|Omi Shrine (Yogo)|ja|乎彌神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lt=Omi Shrine|qq=}}*, which incorporated Noimi Shrine. Additionally, he is also enshrined at *{{ill|Ōfura Shrine|ja|意富布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōfura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* as a subordinate deity.
== Genealogy == Nashitomi no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Kamikikikatsu|lt=Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto|ja|神聞勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}}. His descendants include prominent clans such as the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}}, {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}}.
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}* identifies him as the ancestor of the Kawamatauji no Muraji of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Radical 25|en|Radical 25|WD=Q94724|lt=Radical 25|1=Radical 25}} * {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|clan Urabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}
